name: geefetch

metadata.name: geefetch
metadata.summary: A Python library/CLI to download large scale satellite data from Google Earth Engine.

documentation:
![Code style: black](https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg) ![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/geefetch/badge/?version=latest)

A python library/CLI to download large scale satellite data from Google Earth Engine. ⚠️ This library is still in its early days and in active development. ⚠️ ## Overview ```bash $ geefetch --help Usage: geefetch [OPTIONS] COMMAND [ARGS]... Download satellites from Google Earth Engine. Options: -v, --verbose --quiet / --no-quiet --logfile PATH File to output the log messages in addition to stdout/stderr. --debug --help Show this message and exit. Commands: all Download all satellites given in the config. dynworld Download Dynamic World images. gedi Download GEDI images. s1 Download Sentinel-1 images. s2 Download Sentinel-2 images. landsat8 Download Landsat-8 images. palsar2 Download Palsar-2 images. ``` Currently are supported - [Sentinel-1](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/COPERNICUS_S1_GRD) - [Sentinel-2](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/sentinel-2) - [GEDI](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/LARSE_GEDI_GEDI02_A_002_MONTHLY) - [Dynamic World](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/GOOGLE_DYNAMICWORLD_V1) - [Landsat-8](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/LANDSAT_LC08_C02_T2_L2) - [Palsar-2](https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/datasets/catalog/JAXA_ALOS_PALSAR-2_Level2_2_ScanSAR#description) Read the [docs](https://geefetch.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) for more details.

modules.name: geefetch.data.process
documentation: This module contains data post-processing functions.

functions.name: geefetch.data.process.tif_is_clean
documentation: Check that a 'tif' file is valid and not full of NODATA.

functions.name: geefetch.data.process.gedi_is_clean
documentation: Check if the rasterized gedi at location `path` is not full of NODATA.

functions.name: geefetch.data.process.vector_is_clean
documentation: Check if the geojson file at location `path` is not empty.

functions.name: geefetch.data.process.clean
documentation:
Remove ill-formed and empty data files.

# Parameters

tracker : TileTracker
is_clean: Callable\[[Path], bool\]

> The filtering function, called on every registered tile to determined
> whether it should be kept.

max_threads: int

: Number of threads to use for processing the tiler.

# Returns

int

: The number of removed files.

:::{deprecated} 0.4.0 `clean` will be removed in GeeFetch 0.5.0.
:::

classes.name: geefetch.data.tiler.Tiler

methods.name: geefetch.data.tiler.Tiler.is_on_distortion_overlap
documentation:
Determines if `bbox` is on a tile overlap, because of the way the Tiler splits
large areas.

Parameters
----------
bbox: GeoBoundingBox
    The bbox to check for overlap.

Returns
-------
bool
    When True, the point may be included in several tiles. When False, the point is
    guaranteed not to lie on an overlap.

methods.name: geefetch.data.tiler.Tiler.split
documentation:
Split a region into non-overlapping tiles having shape `shape`x`shape`.

Parameters
----------
aoi : GeoBoundingBox
    The area of interest.
shape : int
    The desired side length for tiles (in meters).
crs : CRS | None
    The CRS in which to download data. If None, AOI is split in UTM zones and
    data is downloaded in their local UTM zones. Defaults to None.
filter_polygon : shapely.Polygon | None
    If given, only yields tiles which WGS84 bounding boxes intersect the polygon.
    Defaults to None.

Yields
------
GeoBoundingBox
The class for handling splitting a region into non-overlapping smaller tiles.

classes.name: geefetch.data.tiler.TileTracker

methods.name: geefetch.data.tiler.TileTracker.root
documentation: The root directory where data is stored.

methods.name: geefetch.data.tiler.TileTracker.satellite
documentation: The satellite associated to the data that the dataset handles.

methods.name: geefetch.data.tiler.TileTracker.tile_id_from_filename
documentation:
Determine a bounding box identifier of a georeferenced file based on its name.
Useful to match file using the file names instead of trusting the georeferenced meta info.

Fails if the filename was not generated using a TileTracker.

methods.name: geefetch.data.tiler.TileTracker.satellite_from_filename
documentation:
Determine a bounding box identifier of a georeferenced file based on its name.
Useful to match file using the file names instead of trusting the georeferenced meta info.

Fails if the filename was not generated using a TileTracker.
The class for handling the interface between data and physical locations in the filesystem.

functions.name: geefetch.data.get._create_vrts
documentation: Create .vrt files for the tracked tif files.

functions.name: geefetch.data.get.download_chip_ts
documentation: Download a specific chip of data from the satellite.
@retry(exceptions=DownloadError, tries=5)

functions.name: geefetch.data.get.download_chip
documentation: Download a specific chip of data from the satellite.

functions.name: geefetch.data.get.download_time_series
documentation:
Download images from a specific satellite. Images are written in several .tif chips
to `dir`. Additionally, a file `.vrt` is written to combine all the chips.

Parameters
----------
data_dir : Path
    Directory or file name to write the downloaded files to. If a directory,
    the default `satellite` name is used as a base name.
bbox : GeoBoundingBox
    The box defining the region of interest.
satellite : SatelliteABC
    The satellite which the images should originate from.
start_date : str
    The start date of the time period of interest.
end_date : str
    The end date of the time period of interest.
selected_bands : list[str] | None
    The bands to download. If None, the default satellite bands are used.
crs : CRS | None
    The CRS in which to download data. If None, AOI is split in UTM zones and
    data is downloaded in their local UTM zones. Defaults to None.
resolution : int
    Resolution of the downloaded data, in meters. Defaults to 10.
tile_shape : int
    Side length of a downloaded chip, in pixels. Defaults to 500.
max_tile_size : int
    Parameter adjusting the memory consumption in Google Earth Engine, in Mb.
    Choose the highest possible that doesn't raise a User Memory Excess error.
    Defaults to 10 Mb.
satellite_get_kwargs : dict[str, Any] | None
    Satellite-dependent parameters for getting data. Defaults to None.
satellite_download_kwargs : dict[str, Any] | None
    Satellite-dependent parameters for downloading data. Defaults to None.
check_clean : bool
    Whether to check if the data is clean. Defaults to True.
filter_polygon : shapely.Polygon | None
    More fine-grained AOI than `bbox`. Defaults to None.
**kwargs : Any
    Accepted but ignored additional arguments.

functions.name: geefetch.data.get.download
documentation:
Download images from a specific satellite. Images are written in several .tif chips
to `dir`. Additionally, a file `.vrt` is written to combine all the chips.

# Parameters

data_dir

: Directory or file name to write the downloaded files to. If a directory,
  the default `satellite` name is used as a base name.

bbox

: The box defining the region of interest.

satellite

: The satellite which the images should originate from.

start_date

: The start date of the time period of interest.

end_date

: The end date of the time period of interest.

selected_bands

: The bands to download. If None, the default satellite bands are used.
  Defaults to None.

crs

: The CRS in which to download data. If None, AOI is split in UTM zones and
  data is downloaded in their local UTM zones. Defaults to None.

resolution

: Resolution of the downloaded data, in meters. Defaults to 10.

tile_shape

: Side length of a downloaded chip, in pixels. Defaults to 500.

max_tile_size

: Parameter adjusting the memory consumption in Google Earth Engine, in Mb.
  Choose the highest possible that doesn't raise a User Memory Excess error.
  Defaults to 10 Mb.

satellite_get_kwargs

: Satellite-dependent parameters for getting data. Defaults to None.

satellite_download_kwargs

: Satellite-dependent parameters for downloading data. Defaults to None.

check_clean

: Whether to check if the data is clean. Defaults to True.

filter_polygon

: More fine-grained AOI than `bbox`. Defaults to None.

in_parallel

: Whether to send parallel download requests. Do not use if the download backend
  is already threaded (e.g., {class}`geefetch.data.downloadable.geedim`). Defaults to False.

max_workers

: How many parallel workers are used in case `in_parallel` is True. Defaults to 10.

functions.name: geefetch.data.get.download_gedi
documentation:
Download GEDI images fused as rasters. Images are written in several .tif chips
to `data_dir`. Additionally, a file `gedi.vrt` is written to combine all the chips.

Parameters
----------
data_dir : Path
    Directory to write the downloaded files to.
bbox : GeoBoundingBox
    The box defining the region of interest.
start_date : str
    The start date of the time period of interest.
end_date : str
    The end date of the time period of interest.
selected_bands : list[str] | None
    The bands to download. If None, the default satellite bands are used.
crs : CRS | None
    The CRS in which to download data. If None, AOI is split in UTM zones and
    data is downloaded in their local UTM zones. Defaults to None.
resolution : int
    Resolution of the downloaded data, in meters. Defaults to 10.
tile_shape : int
    Side length of a downloaded chip, in pixels. Defaults to 500.
max_tile_size : int
    Parameter adjusting the memory consumption in Google Earth Engine, in Mb.
    Choose the highest possible that doesn't raise a User Memory Excess error. Defaults to 10.
composite_method : CompositeMethod
    The composite method to mosaic the image collection. Can be CompositeMethod.TIMESERIES to
    download data as a time series instead of turning it into a mosaic.
    Defaults to CompositeMethod.MEDIAN.
dtype : DType
    The data type of the downloaded images. Defaults to DType.Float32.
filter_polygon : shapely.Polygon | None
    More fine-grained AOI than `bbox`. Defaults to None.

functions.name: geefetch.data.get.download_gedi_vector
documentation:
Download GEDI vector points. Points are written in several .geojson files
to `data_dir`.

Parameters
----------
data_dir : Path
    Directory to write the downloaded files to.
bbox : GeoBoundingBox
    The box defining the region of interest.
start_date : str
    The start date of the time period of interest.
end_date : str
    The end date of the time period of interest.
selected_bands : list[str] | None
    The bands to download. If None, the default satellite bands are used.
crs : CRS | None
    The CRS in which to download data. If None, AOI is split in UTM zones and
    data is downloaded in their local UTM zones. Defaults to None.
tile_shape : int
    Side length of a downloaded chip, in pixels. Defaults to 500.
resolution : int
    Resolution of the downloaded data, in meters. Defaults to 10.
filter_polygon : shapely.Polygon | None
    More fine-grained AOI than `bbox`. Defaults to None.
format : Format
    Format in which to save the vector points. Defaults to Format.CSV.

functions.name: geefetch.data.get.download_s1
documentation:
Download Sentinel-1 images. Images are written in several .tif chips
to `data_dir`. Additionally, a file `s1.vrt` is written to combine all the chips.

Parameters
----------
data_dir : Path
    Directory to write the downloaded files to.
bbox : GeoBoundingBox
    The box defining the region of interest.
start_date : str
    The start date of the time period of interest.
end_date : str
    The end date of the time period of interest.
selected_bands : list[str] | None
    The bands to download. If None, the default satellite bands are used.
crs : CRS | None
    The CRS in which to download data. If None, AOI is split in UTM zones and
    data is downloaded in their local UTM zones. Defaults to None.
resolution : int
    Resolution of the downloaded data, in meters. Defaults to 10.
tile_shape : int
    Side length of a downloaded chip, in pixels. Defaults to 500.
max_tile_size : int
    Parameter adjusting the memory consumption in Google Earth Engine, in Mb.
    Choose the highest possible that doesn't raise a User Memory Excess error. Defaults to 10.
composite_method : CompositeMethod
    The composite method to mosaic the image collection. Can be CompositeMethod.TIMESERIES to
    download data as a time series instead of turning it into a mosaic.
    Defaults to CompositeMethod.MEDIAN.
dtype : DType
    The data type of the downloaded images. Defaults to DType.Float32.
filter_polygon : shapely.Polygon | None
    More fine-grained AOI than `bbox`. Defaults to None.
orbit : S1Orbit
    The orbit used to filter Sentinel-1 images. Defaults to S1Orbit.ASCENDING.

functions.name: geefetch.data.get.download_s2
documentation:
Download Sentinel-2 images. Images are written in several .tif chips
to `data_dir`. Additionally, a file `s2.vrt` is written to combine all the chips.

# Parameters

data_dir

: Directory to write the downloaded files to.

bbox

: The box defining the region of interest.

start_date

: The start date of the time period of interest.

end_date

: The end date of the time period of interest.

selected_bands

: The bands to download. If None, the default satellite bands are used.

crs

: The CRS in which to download data. If None, AOI is split in UTM zones and
  data is downloaded in their local UTM zones. Defaults to None.

resolution

: Resolution of the downloaded data, in meters. Defaults to 10.

tile_shape

: Side length of a downloaded chip, in pixels. Defaults to 500.

max_tile_size

: Parameter adjusting the memory consumption in Google Earth Engine, in Mb.
  Choose the highest possible that doesn't raise a User Memory Excess error. Defaults to 10.

composite_method

: The composite method to mosaic the image collection. Can be CompositeMethod.TIMESERIES to
  download data as a time series instead of turning it into a mosaic.
  Defaults to CompositeMethod.MEDIAN.

dtype

: The data type of the downloaded images. Defaults to DType.Float32.

filter_polygon

: More fine-grained AOI than `bbox`. Defaults to None.

cloudless_portion

: Portion of the image expected to be cloudless.
  See {meth}`geefetch.data.s2.get`. Defaults to 60.

cloud_prb_thresh

: Cloud probability threshold. See {meth}`geefetch.data.s2.get`. Defaults to 40.

documentation:
Download Dynamic World images. Images are written in several .tif chips

resolution : int
    Resolution of the downloaded data, in meters. Defaults to 10.
tile_shape : int
    Side length of a downloaded chip, in pixels. Defaults to 500.
max_tile_size : int
    Parameter adjusting the memory consumption in Google Earth Engine, in Mb.
    Choose the highest possible that doesn't raise a User Memory Excess error. Defaults to 10.
composite_method : CompositeMethod
    The composite method to mosaic the image collection. Can be CompositeMethod.TIMESERIES to
    download data as a time series instead of turning it into a mosaic.
    Defaults to CompositeMethod.MEDIAN.
dtype : DType

tile_shape : int
    Side length of a downloaded chip, in pixels. Defaults to 500.

    The composite method to mosaic the image collection. Can be CompositeMethod.TIMESERIES to

dtype : DType
    The data type of the downloaded images. Defaults to DType.Float32.
filter_polygon : shapely.Polygon | None
    More fine-grained AOI than `bbox`. Defaults to None.

methods.name: geefetch.data.satellites.palsar2.Palsar2.get_col
documentation:
Get Palsar 2 collection.

Parameters
----------
aoi : GeoBoundingBox
    Area of interest.
start_date : str
    Start date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
end_date : str
    End date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
orbit : P2Orbit
    The orbit used to filter the collection before mosaicking.

Returns
-------
palsar2_col : ee.ImageCollection

methods.name: geefetch.data.satellites.palsar2.Palsar2.get_time_series
documentation:
Get Palsar-2 collection.

Parameters
----------
aoi : GeoBoundingBox
    Area of interest.
start_date : str
    Start date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
end_date : str
    End date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
dtype : DType
    The data type for the image
orbit : P2Orbit
    The orbit used to filter the collection before mosaicking.
**kwargs : Any
    Accepted but ignored additional arguments.

Returns
-------
p2_im: DownloadableGeedimImageCollection
    A Palsar-2 time series collection of the specified AOI and time range.

methods.name: geefetch.data.satellites.s2.S2.get_col
documentation:
Get Sentinel-2 cloud free collection.

Parameters
----------
aoi : GeoBoundingBox
    Area of interest.
start_date : str
    Start date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
end_date : str
    End date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
cloudless_portion : int
    Threshold for the portion of filled pixels that must be cloud/shadow free (%).
    Images that do not fullfill the requirement are filtered out. Defaults to 60.
cloud_prb_thresh : int
    Threshold for cloud probability above which a pixel is filtered out (%).
    Defaults to 30.

Returns
-------
s2_cloudless : ee.ImageCollection

methods.name: geefetch.data.satellites.s2.S2.get_time_series
documentation:
Get Sentinel-2 collection.

Parameters
----------
aoi : GeoBoundingBox
    Area of interest.
start_date : str
    Start date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
end_date : str
    End date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
dtype: DType
    The data type for the image.
cloudless_portion : int
    Threshold for the portion of filled pixels that must be cloud/shadow free (%).
    Images that do not fullfill the requirement are filtered out.
cloud_prb_thresh : int
    Threshold for cloud probability above which a pixel is filtered out (%).
**kwargs : Any
    Accepted but ignored additional arguments.

Returns
-------
s2_im: DownloadableGeedimImageCollection
    A Sentinel-2 time series collection of the specified AOI and time range.

methods.name: geefetch.data.satellites.s2.S2.get
documentation:
Get Sentinel-2 cloud free collection.

Parameters
----------
aoi : GeoBoundingBox
    Area of interest.
start_date : str
    Start date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
end_date : str
    End date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.
composite_method: CompositeMethod
dtype: DType
    The data type for the image.
cloudless_portion : int
    Threshold for the portion of filled pixels that must be cloud/shadow free (%).
    Images that do not fullfill the requirement are filtered out.
cloud_prb_thresh : int
    Threshold for cloud probability above which a pixel is filtered out (%).
buffer : float
    Kernel size to dilate cloud/shadow patches.
**kwargs : Any
    Accepted but ignored additional arguments.

Returns
-------
s2_im : DownloadableGeedimImage
    A Sentinel-2 composite image of the specified AOI and time range,
    with clouds filtered out.

methods.name: geefetch.data.satellites.abc.SatelliteABC.name

methods.name: geefetch.data.satellites.abc.SatelliteABC.full_name

methods.name: geefetch.data.satellites.abc.SatelliteABC.pixel_range
documentation:
The minimum and maximum values that pixels can take.

When converting the image to another type, pixels outside of that value range will saturate.

Abstract base class for a satellite class, describing how to obtain data and various metadata
about the satellite.

modules.name: geefetch.data
documentation:
This subpackage is designed to facilitate the downloading of satellite data from
Google Earth Engine (GEE).

It provides abstract base classes for defining download interfaces and satellite metadata,
as well as concrete implementations with sane defaults for some Google Earth Engine datasets.
It is easily extensible to support other GEE datasets.

It also provides functions to download composite or raw time series data.

documentation: Utilites to retrieve an image collection of GEDI images, with bad data points filtered out.

Get GEDI collection.

aoi : GeoBoundingBox
    Area of interest.

    Start date in "YYYY-MM-DD" format.

modules.name: geefetch.data.downloadables.image
documentation:
[LEGACY CODE]
This module provides downloading utility functions for Google Earth Engine's Image,
similar to what `geedim` provides for Image and ImageCollection.

methods.name: geefetch.data.downloadables.image.DownloadableGEEImage._get_download_url
documentation: Get tile download url and response.

methods.name: geefetch.data.downloadables.image.DownloadableGEEImage.download
documentation:
Download a GEE Image in one go.
It is up to the caller to make sure that the image does not exceed
Google Earth Engine compute limit.

Parameters
----------
out : Path
    Path to the geojson file to download the collection to.
region : GeoBoundingBox
    The ROI.
crs : CRS
    The CRS to use for the features' geometries.
bands : list[str]
    Properties of the collection to select for download.
**kwargs : Any
    Accepted but ignored additional arguments.

methods.name: geefetch.data.downloadables.abc.DownloadableABC.download
documentation:
Download data.

Parameters
----------
out : Path
    The file to download the data to.
region : GeoBoundingBox
    The AOI.
crs : CRS
    The CRS in which `region` is expressed and in which to express the data.
bands : list[str]
    The bands (for images) or properties (for collections) to select for download.
**kwargs : Any
    Any additional necessary arguments.

modules.name: geefetch
documentation:
geefetch
========

A python library/CLI to download large scale satellite data from Google Earth Engine.

methods.name: geefetch.data.downloadables.collection.DownloadableGEECollection.download

Download a FeatureCollection in one go.
It is up to the caller to make sure that the collection does not exceed
Google Earth Engine compute limit.

    Properties of the collection to select for download.

    The desired filetype.
**kwargs : Any
    Accepted but ignored additional arguments.

modules.name: geefetch.cli
documentation: The CLI exposed by GeeFetch, with a structured configuration powered by Omegaconfig.

functions.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.post_omegaconf_load
documentation:
Updates in place the missing satellites config with the default.

Parameters
----------
config : DictConfig
    The config loaded by OmegaConf.

functions.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.load
documentation: Load a config file.

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.GEEConfig
documentation:
Configuration of Google Earth Engine.

Attributes
----------
ee_project_id : str
    Your GEE id, to connect to the API.

    .. see also:: https://developers.google.com/earth-engine/apidocs/ee-initialize
max_tile_size : int
    Size constraint in MB for the request sent to GEE. This is heuristical and depends
    in general on what satellite you are interested in.
    Decrease if User Memory Excess Error, but choose highest possible otherwise. Defaults is 10.

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.SpatialAOIConfig
documentation:
Configuration of the spatial area of interest.

Attributes
----------
left : float
right : float
top : float
bottom : float
epsg : int
    EPSG code for the CRS in which the boundaries are given. If given,
    the downloaded data will be expressed in that same CRS.
    Defaults is 4326, corresponding to WGS84 (latitude, longitude).

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.TemporalAOIConfig
documentation:
Configuration of the temporal range of interest.

Attributes
----------
start_date : str
    Start date in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format.
end_date : str
    End date in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format.

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.AOIConfig
documentation:
Configuration of a spatial/temporal Area of Interest (AOI).

Attributes
----------
spatial : SpatialAOIConfig
temporal : TemporalAOIConfig
country : str | None
    The name of a country. If given, spatial AOI is further restricted to its area
    that intersects the country boundaries. Defaults to None.

    .. note:: See https://www.naturalearthdata.com/downloads/110m-cultural-vectors/
        for possible values

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.SatelliteDefaultConfig
documentation:
The structured type for a GeeFetch default satellite configuration

Attributes
----------
aoi : AOIConfig
    The temporal/spatial Area of Interest
gee : GEEConfig
    Google Earth Engine specific configurationss
tile_size : int
    The pixel side length for downloaded images
resolution : int
    The resolution for downloaded images, in meters
dtype : DType
    The data type for downloaded images. Can be used to
    reduce file size and download speed at the cost of
    some loss of precision.
composite_method : CompositeMethod
    The mosaicking method. Use CompositeMethod.TIMESERIES
    to download time series instead of mosaicks. Defaults
    to CompositeMethod.MEDIAN.
selected_bands : list[str] | None
    The bands to download. If None, will use the satellite
    default bands. Defaults to None.

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.GediConfig
documentation:
The structured type for configuring GEDI.

Attributes
----------
format : Format
    Filetype for downloading vector GEDI. Defaults to Format.PARQUET

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.S1Config
documentation: The structured type for configuring Sentinel-1.

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.S2Config
documentation:
The structured type for configuring Sentinel-2.

Attributes
----------
cloudless_portion : int
    Threshold for the portion of filled pixels that must be cloud/shadow free (%).
    Images that do not fullfill the requirement are filtered out before mosaicking.
cloud_prb_threshold : int
    Threshold for cloud probability above which a pixel is filtered out (%).

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.DynWorldConfig
documentation: The structured type for configuring Dynamic World.

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.Landsat8Config
documentation: The structured type for configuring Landsat 8.

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.Palsar2Config
documentation: The structured type for configuring Landsat 8.

classes.name: geefetch.cli.omegaconfig.GeefetchConfig
documentation:
The structured type for a GeeFetch configuration.

Attributes
----------
data_dir : Path
    The path to store downloaded data.
satellite_default : SatelliteDefaultConfig
    Default satellite configuration.
gedi : GediConfig | None
    GEDI specific configuration / variation to the default.
s1 : S1Config | None
    Sentinel-1 specific configuration / variation to the default.
s2 : S2Config | None
    Sentinel-2 specific configuration / variation to the default.
dynworld : DynWorldConfig | None
    Dynamic world specific configuration / variation to the default.
landsat8 : Landsat8Config | None
    Landsat 8 specific configuration / variation to the default.
palsar2 : Palsar2Config | None
    Palsar 2 specific configuration / variation to the default.

modules.name: geefetch.coords
documentation: Utilities for working with different system of coordinates.

functions.name: geefetch.coords.close_to_utm_border
documentation:
Check if the point at coordinate (lat, lon) is delta-close to a UTM border.

# Parameters

lat

: Lattitude coordinate in WGS84.

lon

: Longitude coordinate in WGS84.

delta

: Distante to a UTM border to check for, in degree. Defaults to 1.

# Returns

bool

:::{deprecated} 0.4.0 `close_to_utm_border` will be removed in GeeFetch 0.5.0.
:::

functions.name: geefetch.coords.get_center_tif
documentation:
Compute the center of a tif image in WGS84 CRS.

# Parameters

ds

: A rio dataset representing a tif image.

# Returns

lat, lon

: Latitude and longitude of the center of `ds`.

# Example

ds = rio.open("example.tif")
m = folium.Map(location=getCenterTif(ds))

:::{deprecated} 0.4.0 `get_center_tif` will be removed in GeeFetch 0.5.0.
:::

functions.name: geefetch.coords.get_shape_image
documentation:
Compute the shape of a GEE image in (width, height) format.

# Parameters

image : ee.Image

# Returns

w, h : tuple[int, int]

:::{deprecated} 0.4.0 `get_shape_image` will be removed in GeeFetch 0.5.0.
:::

functions.name: geefetch.coords.get_bounding_box_tif
documentation:
Compute the bounding box of a tif image in WGS84 CRS.

# Parameters

ds

: A rio dataset representing a tif image.

# Returns

(lat_min, lon_min) : Coordinate
(lat_max, lon_max) : Coordinate

> Coordinates of the top right and bottom left box corners.

:::{deprecated} 0.4.0 `get_bounding_box_tif` will be removed in GeeFetch 0.5.0.
:::
